title: Astragalus canispinus
taxonomy: Astragalus canispinus is a species of legume in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as peas and beans. It belongs to the genus Astragalus, which comprises over 2,000 species of vetches and milkvetches. The plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, where it grows in dry, rocky areas. Astragalus canispinus is a perennial herb with small, pink or purple flowers and is often used in habitat restoration efforts. As a member of the Fabaceae family, it plays a crucial role in nitrogen fixation and soil health.

intro: Astragalus canispinus is a species of perennial legume, native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and providing antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus canispinus include isoflavones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
